text: Being here, I am overwhelmed by a sense of curiosity tinged with unease. The architecture is modern, sterile, yet the presence of cameras and flickering screens makes me acutely aware of being observed. The exhibition’s focus on research and development, connectivity, and adaptation evokes existential thoughts: how do we maintain our individuality in a world that thrives on surveillance and data? I sense a subtle tension—the allure of discovery mingled with the discomfort of exposure. Amidst these vibrant, informative walls, I feel both inspired to question and sensitized to every glance, every whisper in this charged space.
